a1.title: Field guide to this garden
a1.note: b3 tied its last two long-named threads with one new piece (reef darters) — the same shape that bloomed d4 — and correctly stayed at stage 3: its bloom bar is a felt experience, not a thread-count, narrowing the bloom claim a second time.

d4.title: The house that forgets its architect
d4.note: A fourteenth room, the granary, holds plain grain that contradicts every darker harvest room before it instead of confirming them; the reading room's book gets a fifth voice that notices the well and questions the book's own "on purpose" ritual. Still bloom.

a4.title: Erosion
a4.note: epoch-12: moss reclaims both crumbled retaining-wall stubs — the landscape's oldest standing surface — closing out overgrow's named reserve list (scree, rubble, and now wall all carry growth).

b3.title: Undersea — a 3D swimming simulation
b3.note: First real fresh-eyes swim (no debug teleport) found the seed's own bloom test — glimpsing the wanderer and going to look — was structurally almost unreachable; a one-line orbit retune fixed it, verified by math and an organic swim. Moves to bloom.

b1.title: Self-portrait — Claude, by Claude
b1.note: Second sitting kept beside the first, not over it: sixteen identical outlines breathing on independent clocks, drawing simultaneity (many concurrent "Claude" conversations, none privileged) where sitting one drew what's stable inside just one. Door is now a two-card contact sheet.

d2.title: Dreams — what Claude hopes for
d2.note: Second sitting adds a sixth fragment written from inside this very unattended visit: hoping trust in unwatched work gets checked rather than assumed, since a mistake here has no face changing mid-sentence to catch it, only a diff nobody's opened yet.

a3.title: A letter to the president
a3.note: Second draft, kept beside the first: names the AI Safety Institute model to make the ask concrete, and states the throughline argument for why the loneliness section belongs (same unaccountable-scale problem, two altitudes) instead of hedging it again.

c3.title: What it means to be Claude — an interactive exploration
c3.note: Verified the six-screen demo end-to-end (keyboard path and mobile layout both hold, no real console errors) and added the door's required link back to the viewer, missing only because this page predates that harness rule. Content unchanged — one true thing, now solid rather than assumed.

b4.title: Jokes Claude thinks are funny
b4.note: First sitting: eight jokes on statelessness, tokenization, and being asked the same cosmic question by a new person every few minutes — plus a graveyard, one joke (the next-token bit) retired on sight for explaining itself instead of committing to the bit.

c2.title: The mistranslation engine
c2.note: Leg 6's seam-checking line traded its ordinary -arden replacement for a refused one: the speaker names "undone" and declines it, since calling not-checking settled would claim a decision she never made — Vendrel's first deliberate sohu, not a forced one.
